text: The patient underwent a cholecystectomy on March 15, 2016. Post-operative recovery was uneventful. Follow-up appointment scheduled for April 1.
Patient has been taking metformin 500mg twice daily since January 2015. Blood glucose levels have improved significantly over the past 6 months.
Chest X-ray performed on 2016-02-20 revealed bilateral infiltrates. Antibiotics were initiated the same day. Repeat imaging was ordered for one week later.
The patient presented to the emergency department at 3:45 AM with acute chest pain. An ECG was performed immediately and showed ST-segment elevation.
History of hypertension diagnosed in 2010. Currently managed with lisinopril 10mg daily. Blood pressure has been well controlled since starting the medication.
MRI of the lumbar spine performed yesterday showed a herniated disc at L4-L5. Physical therapy was recommended before considering surgical intervention.
Patient completed a 10-day course of amoxicillin for strep throat. Symptoms resolved after 72 hours of treatment. No adverse reactions were reported.
Colonoscopy scheduled for next Tuesday. Patient instructed to begin bowel preparation 24 hours prior. Last colonoscopy was performed in 2013.
The patient was admitted on February 5 with pneumonia. IV antibiotics were started upon admission and continued for 5 days. Discharged on February 10 in stable condition.
Insulin therapy was initiated during the hospitalization in December 2015. The patient has been monitoring blood glucose at home three times daily since discharge.
